answer:
**In the Old Javanese (Kawi) *tattva* texts of the Falsafa corpus—*Gaṇapatitattva*, *Vṛhaspatitattva*, *Saṃ Hyaṃ Tattvajñāna*, and *Saṃ Hyaṃ Mahājñāna*—'param' (Sanskrit *parama*, "supreme") consistently denotes the highest metaphysical principle, *Paramaśiva* or *Paramaśivatattva*. This is the unmanifest (*niṣkala*), eternal, all-pervading summit of the *tattva*s (principles of reality), beyond attributes, activity, duality, and the cycle of manifestation. It transcends even *Sadāśivatattva* (eternal Śiva, active and omniscient) and *Śivatattva* (pure consciousness), representing absolute stability, subtlety, and sovereignty within the *cetana* (conscious) hierarchy.**[^1][^2][^3][^4]

### Core Characteristics Across Texts
- **Supreme *Tattva* in the *Cetana* Series**: *Paramaśivatattva* heads the three conscious principles (*cetana tattva*s): *Paramaśivatattva* > *Sadāśivatattva* > *Śivatattva*. It is *aprameya* (immeasurable), *anirdeśya* (indescribable), *sūkṣma* (subtle), *vyāpaka* (all-pervading), *nitya* (eternal), and *avyaya* (immutable), without qualities, action, or limitation.[^5][^6]
- **Unmanifest and Formless**: The Lord's state in *niṣkala* (formless), without motion, origin, or end; it fills the cosmos yet remains untouched by it.[^7]
- **Mantric and Yogic Apex**: In *Gaṇapatitattva*, *ya* in *Namaḥ Śivāya* symbolizes the *Paramaśiva*-principle; meditation yields liberation. It is the "Living-Self" guiding *hurip* (life), beyond form/color.[^8]
- **Soteriological Goal**: Union with *Paramaśivatattva* via *yoga*/*samādhi* ends *saṃsāra*; it is *paramakaivalya* (supreme isolation), the "supportless" void beyond pleasure/pain.[^9][^10]

These texts synthesize Śaiva ontology, where *parama* resolves the *cetana*-*acetana* (conscious-unconscious) duality into ultimate non-dual reality. No single work exhausts it; it is the shared esoteric pinnacle for yogic ascent.[^11]

[^1]: *Vṛhaspatitattva*, Ch. 1, [para.](/works/unknown-vrhaspatitattva-b60b28/01/translation/#p-c31df3).
[^2]: *Saṃ Hyaṃ Tattvajñāna*, Ch. 1, [para.](/works/unknown-san-hyan-tattvajnana-1f29bd/01/translation/#p-daaeef).
[^3]: *Gaṇapatitattva*, Ch. 1, [para.](/works/unknown-ganapatitattva-66a136/01/translation/#p-da342b).
[^4]: *Saṃ Hyaṃ Mahājñāna*, Ch. 1, [para.](/works/unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531/01/translation/#p-71743d).

[^5]: *Vṛhaspatitattva*, Ch. 1, [paras.](/works/unknown-vrhaspatitattva-b60b28/01/translation/#p-608afe,p-6250fd,p-8703c0).
[^6]: *Saṃ Hyaṃ Tattvajñāna*, Ch. 1, [para.](/works/unknown-san-hyan-tattvajnana-1f29bd/01/translation/#p-34f807).
[^7]: *Saṃ Hyaṃ Tattvajñāna*, Ch. 1, [para.](/works/unknown-san-hyan-tattvajnana-1f29bd/01/translation/#p-34f807).
[^8]: *Gaṇapatitattva*, Ch. 1, [paras.](/works/unknown-ganapatitattva-66a136/01/translation/#p-1f5228,p-4eb79e).
[^9]: *Vṛhaspatitattva*, Ch. 1, [para.](/works/unknown-vrhaspatitattva-b60b28/01/translation/#p-7171d3).
[^10]: *Saṃ Hyaṃ Mahājñāna*, Ch. 1, [para.](/works/unknown-san-hyan-mahajnana-11c531/01/translation/#p-9194a6).
[^11]: *Saṃ Hyaṃ Tattvajñāna*, Ch. 1, [whole chapter](/works/unknown-san-hyan-tattvajnana-1f29bd/01/translation/).
